§ 01Introduction

Dear {{trusteeName}},

Forhemit Transition Stewardship ("Forhemit") is writing to introduce a transaction engagement and to inquire about your availability to serve as the independent ESOP trustee for {{companyName}}.

Forhemit is a transaction stewardship firm that facilitates employee ownership transitions. We coordinate the professional team, manage the process timeline, and provide post-close operational stewardship. We are not a buyer, broker, attorney, or financial advisor, and we are not an ERISA fiduciary.

§ 02Deal Summary
Transaction Overview
{{companyName}}
{{industry}}
{{location}}
{{entityType}}
{{ebitda}}
{{valuation}}
{{employeeCount}}
100% ESOP — new plan
{{sbaFinancing}}
{{currentStage}}
§ 03Trustee Role & Scope

The independent ESOP trustee will be responsible for:

A.FMV Determination. Engaging an independent appraiser and reviewing the adequacy of the fair market value opinion.
B.Fiduciary Review. Evaluating whether the transaction is fair to the ESOP and its participants from a financial and fiduciary standpoint.
C.LOI & Purchase Agreement. Reviewing and negotiating the letter of intent and stock purchase agreement on behalf of the ESOP.
D.Closing. Executing the transaction documents and directing the ESOP's participation at closing.
E.Post-Close Oversight. Ongoing fiduciary oversight of the ESOP following the transaction.
§ 04Forhemit's Role (and What It Is Not)

To be clear about the boundaries:

✓Forhemit IS: The transaction coordinator, lender package preparer, timeline manager, and post-close stewardship provider.
✗Forhemit IS NOT: The buyer, the seller's agent, an ERISA fiduciary, a legal advisor, or a tax advisor. Forhemit does not direct the trustee's independent judgment.

The trustee is engaged by and reports to the ESOP — not to Forhemit, not to the seller, and not to the company. Forhemit's role is to provide the trustee with organized deal materials, coordinate timing, and ensure the transaction process moves efficiently.

§ 05What We Need From You

If you are available and interested, we request the following:

1.Conflict check. Please confirm that neither you nor your firm has an existing relationship with {{companyName}}, its owners, or its counsel that would create a conflict.
2.Availability. Please confirm your availability to begin substantive work within {{timelineWeeks}} weeks of engagement.
3.Fee indication. Please provide your standard fee range for a transaction of this size and complexity.
4.Experience confirmation. Please confirm your experience with {{entityType}}-to-C-Corp ESOP transitions, if applicable.
Next step: Please reply to deals@forhemit.com with your conflict check results and availability. If clear, we will provide the full deal package for your review.
§ 06Contact

For questions about this engagement or the transaction, please contact:

deals@forhemit.com

Please reference the above number in all correspondence related to this engagement.

—Forhemit Transition Stewardship
